steps.title: Find the boundary line
steps.content: From the graph, the solid boundary line passes through $(0,-6)$ and $(9,0)$. The slope is $\frac{0-(-6)}{9-0}=\frac{6}{9}=\frac{2}{3}$, and the $y$-intercept is $-6$, giving boundary $y=\frac{2}{3}x-6$.

steps.title: Determine the inequality direction
steps.content: The shaded region lies above the line and the line itself is solid (included), so the inequality is $y\ge\frac{2}{3}x-6$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y\ge\frac{2}{3}x-6$ — correct slope and correct $y$-intercept of $-6$. Matches.
  • B) $y\ge\frac{2}{3}x+6$ — $y$-intercept of $+6$ does not match the graph. Eliminated.
  • C) $y\ge\frac{2}{3}x-9$ — $y$-intercept of $-9$ does not match the graph. Eliminated.
  • D) $y\ge\frac{2}{3}x+9$ — $y$-intercept of $+9$ does not match. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
